title: Lemongrass Honey
text:
I want to be known for who I am,
And not boxed into who I could be.
I don’t want to feel like a program;
I want to be completely set free.
I want to be fully understood,
For what I think and what I do.
I want to be reassured,
That they understand my point of view.
I want to be deeply cherished
Like lemongrass and honey
Will you say this before I am perished?
Will I ever be somebody?
To be loved is to be known;
And to be known is to be loved.
Can you say that without hesitation?
Or do you have to be shoved?
I want to be lemongrass honey:
Sour with a kick of sweetness,
Head empty, sometimes funny,
Only then can I feel complete.

title: Girlnough
text:
I could wake up in the morning,
Wash my face and slap on lotion,
Patiently wait for it to dry,
While folding my cleaned laundry.
I could clip my hair with cute pins,
Sharpen my eyebrows with tweezers,
Trim my bangs just below my eyes,
Curl my hair into bouncy Js.
I could look almost effortless,
Use concealer as foundation,
Cherry pink under my eyebags,
Add eyeshadow as eyeliner.
I could wear off the shoulder tops,
Paired with high-waisted baggy jeans,
Chunky sneakers to finish it,
Now I almost look like a girl.
I could smile if they glance at me,
Ask them questions about their day,
Be interested in their life,
I almost blend in with the girls.
I suppose I could do much more,
In fact, I can’t help but compare,
How they are well groomed and pretty,
So why don’t I feel pretty too?
Their beauty and my beauty clash,
Different aesthetics and preference,
I’m trying to play on their home field,
It’s no wonder I feel awkward.
Maybe I don’t need to blend in,
Or find out what is wrong with me.
I don’t need their type of pretty,
I want mine to make me alive.

title: Poems
text: Pieces of writing from when I was inspired.

title: Mika Kohno
text:
Hello! I’m an undergrad at Cal Poly SLO.
I write poems and occasionally post thoughts or small observations that don’t really fit anywhere.
This is a space for those fragments, ranging from writing, ideas, and whatever else inspires me over time. Or in other words: a place where poetry, thought, and learning coexist without hierarchy.



Snoopy from Peanuts by Charles M. Schulz


Created April 17, 2026.
